questions.question: Which city was originally selected to host the 1940 Summer Olympics before forfeiting the event due to the Second Sino-Japanese War?
questions.explanation: Tokyo was awarded the 1940 Games but forfeited them in 1938. They were then reassigned to Helsinki before being cancelled entirely due to World War II.

presentation.questionStyled: Before the world went up in flames, which city was originally tapped to host the 1940 Summer Olympics before reality intervened?
presentation.explanationStyled: It was indeed Tokyo, though they wisely forfeited in 1938. Helsinki got the hand-me-down, before the entire event was scrapped for obvious, catastrophic reasons.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Let us see if your grasp of history is as shaky as the 1940 schedule.
wrongAnswerQuips: A charming thought, but absolutely incorrect.
Helsinki was merely the backup plan, try harder.
Rome? You are clearly lost in the wrong century.
London wasn't even in the running for that disaster.

questions.question: Which 1984 science fiction film was the first to use integrated computer-generated imagery (CGI) for all of its space combat scenes instead of physical models?
questions.explanation: The Last Starfighter was a pioneer in cinema for using digital renders from a Cray X-MP supercomputer to replace traditional scale models for its space sequences.

presentation.questionStyled: Which 1984 sci-fi flick ditched physical models for digital trickery, marking the first time computers handled the heavy lifting for space combat?
presentation.explanationStyled: The answer is The Last Starfighter. It leaned on a Cray X-MP supercomputer to prove that physical models were quickly becoming a relic of the past.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Time to test your knowledge of vintage digital pioneers, assuming you can recall anything.
wrongAnswerQuips: Tron? Everyone always guesses Tron. How predictable.
Starman? That is just a lovely piece of fiction.
Dune. A noble guess, but entirely wrong.

questions.question: Despite their global success in the disco era and their Caribbean-inspired sound, the group Boney M. was formed in which country?
questions.explanation: Boney M. was created by German record producer Frank Farian in 1975 and was based in West Germany throughout its peak success.

presentation.questionStyled: Despite that tropical vibe, Boney M. was not exactly island-born. In which country was this disco outfit actually formed?
presentation.explanationStyled: The group was the brainchild of German producer Frank Farian in 1975, basing the entire operation in West Germany. Sorry to burst your sunny bubble.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Surely you haven't been fooled by a little disco aesthetic, have you?
wrongAnswerQuips: Common sense is clearly not your strong suit today.
The UK? You really don't pay attention, do you?
Netherlands? A valiant effort at guessing, but no.
